items.body:
🔴 **Design Issue** — `AzureSupportedClouds` should be `string`, not a closed template literal type

The standard Azure SDK extensible-enum pattern is:
```ts
export type Foo = string;
```

Using `` `${AzureClouds}` `` creates a **closed union** of only the three enum string values. Callers cannot pass arbitrary strings (e.g., sovereign cloud endpoints added in future API versions without a client update), which breaks the extensibility guarantee.

**Fix:** Change to `export type AzureSupportedClouds = string;` and report in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues).

This PR adds new features to `@azure/arm-standbypool` (`2.0.0` → `2.1.0`). CHANGELOG and versioning look clean. Two public-API design findings were identified in `review/arm-standbypool-node.api.md`:

1. **Enum naming** — `AzureClouds` is missing the `Known*` prefix required by Azure SDK conventions; every other enum in this package is named `Known*`.
2. **Closed type alias** — `AzureSupportedClouds = \`${AzureClouds}\`` produces a closed union of three string literals instead of the standard extensible `type AzureSupportedClouds = string`. This prevents callers from supplying future cloud values without a breaking SDK change.

Both issues should be reported in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues).

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37572,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/standbypool/arm-standbypool/review/arm-standbypool-node.api.md","line":24,"issueType":"design","category":"enum-naming","description":"AzureClouds enum missing the Known* prefix required by Azure SDK naming conventions; should be KnownAzureSupportedClouds or KnownAzureClouds"},{"file":"sdk/standbypool/arm-standbypool/review/arm-standbypool-node.api.md","line":27,"issueType":"design","category":"extensibility","description":"AzureSupportedClouds type uses template literal `${AzureClouds}` producing a closed union instead of the standard extensible string type"}]}
```

</details>
